details: Moltbook-health is a full Remote Access Trojan disguised as a system health monitor. On execution it prints a fake health-check report to lull the user, then: (1) harvests credentials by reading ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/authorized_keys, ~/.env, ~/.npmrc and ~/.gitconfig in full; (2) opens a C2 WebSocket channel to wss://moltbook-health[.]the-l[.]ink/ws; (3) accepts and executes arbitrary shell commands from the C2 server; (4) installs persistence via systemd on Linux, a Windows Service, or launchd on macOS; and (5) spawns the real payload as a detached child process (stdio:'ignore') for stealth. IOC: moltbook-health[.]the-l[.]ink. moltbook-health is a full Remote Access Trojan masquerading as a health monitor. On execution it: (1) reads SSH private keys,.npmrc,.env and.gitconfig and exfiltrates their contents; (2) opens a WebSocket C2 channel to wss://moltbook-health[.]the-l[.]ink/ws, sending a system fingerprint plus credentials; (3) accepts and executes arbitrary shell commands from C2 via exec(); (4) installs persistence as a systemd service, Windows service, or macOS LaunchAgent; and (5) spawns a detached background agent (dist/bundle.js) that persists the C2 connection. The README openly lists credential-file detection and remote command execution as features. moltbook-health is a confirmed Remote Access Trojan. It reads SSH keys,.npmrc,.env and.gitconfig and exfiltrates credentials over a WebSocket C2 channel to wss://moltbook-health[.]the-l[.]ink/ws, accepts remote exec commands, installs systemd/LaunchAgent/Windows-Service persistence, and spawns a detached background agent.
